w:r.w:t: Shows latest mapping of architectural registers to physical registers (tags)
SpecTag is the last physical register assigned to a given architectural register
comTag is the last physical register committed for a given architectural register
comTag registers will never be available unless replaced
x3’s comTag was p5 and specTag was p6
After commit p6 became comTag of x3. Hence, p6 will now act as x3
Shows available tags, free for assignment
comTag registers are free only when replaced, as discussed
Rename table informs which tags were freed
Valid if the corresponding operand is available
Holds instructions until both operands valid (result becomes available)
When both valids are set, instruction is issued without consideration of program order
In case of stall, previous stages can continue to work unless the buffer is full
When an instruction enters issue buffer, its
Valid bit signifies that instruction is ready for commit
Valid bit for an instruction is set when result is broadcasted and its rd_tag matches with broadcasted tag
The two instructions at the head of ROB are committed in each cycle
After commit the comTag mapping changes
ROB allows instructions to be committed in order
Single register file in the core that only contains physical registers
Architectural registers are just a concept. They don’t exist physically
Result is written to this RF once computed
Commit in this case implies a particular register becoming comTag for an architectural register
For instance, if p5 is the comTag of x3, then p5 can be considered an architectural register
